concerns.text: T021 (Data Cleaning) is placed in Phase 5 (US2) but logically depends on T019 (Raw Data Logging) in Phase 4 (US1). The analysis pipeline cannot execute cleaning (T021) before the raw data artifact (data/raw/session_{id}.json) is produced by T019. The current ordering implies US2 can start before US1 is complete, violating the 'Producer before Consumer' rule for the data artifact.

T027 (Visualization) and T028 (Notebook) are placed in Phase 6 (US3) but depend on the statistical summary output generated by T026 (Phase 5). The current structure correctly groups them by story, but the 'Dependencies & Execution Order' section must explicitly state that Phase 6 cannot begin until Phase 5 (T026) completes, as the visualizer consumes the `metrics_summary.csv` produced by T026.

T012 (App Entry Point) and T018 (App Integration) are listed in Phase 3 and 4 respectively. However, T018 explicitly 'integrates all collectors' into the app. If T012 creates the entry point without the collectors (which are implemented in T016-T017), the app will be non-functional. T012 should be moved to Phase 4 (after T016/T017) or split into 'Skeleton App' (Phase 3) and 'Integrated App' (Phase 4) to respect the dependency that the app flow requires the collector implementations.

T007 creates 'base data models'. T019 (Phase 4) logs data to JSON. The dependency is implicit. To ensure strict ordering, T007 should be explicitly noted as a prerequisite for T019 in the 'Dependencies' section, as the logging task relies on the model structure defined in T007 to serialize the session data correctly.
